page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-144 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 144 vCPUs and 288 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 72 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a substantial 504 MB L3 cache, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multi-core compute and memory bandwidth workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory performance is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block bandwidth reaching 11,546.73 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 543.81 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c4-highcpu-144 is designed for compute-bound workloads rather than memory-heavy applications. It demonstrates exceptional throughput in static web serving and database operations, alongside top-tier performance in CPU-based LLM prompt processing. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance as a highly cost-efficient choice for raw CPU-intensive processing, software compilation, and parallelized mathematical simulations where external storage or network-attached disks are utilized.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-144 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 144 vCPUs and 288 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with 72 physical cores and hyper-threading, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The processor features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is tailored for high-density CPU workloads. Benchmarks show exceptional results in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations. It is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, including static web serving, parallel simulations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-144 is a compute-optimized server with 144 vCPUs, 288 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-144 is a compute-optimized instance with 144 vCPUs and 288 GB RAM, built for high-performance CPU workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized instance featuring 144 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 144 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors.
288 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
Hardware-accelerated execution via Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
No local storage or GPU resources included.
